title: Explaining Online Reinforcement Learning Decisions of Self-Adaptive Systems
abstract: Not explicitly provided in the text.

methodology.description: A combined technique that overcomes the respective limitations of the individual techniques.
methodology.evaluation: Qualitative and quantitative results of applying XRL-DINE to a self-adaptive system exemplar

results: Online RL means that reinforcement learning is employed at runtime
The self-adaptive system can learn from actual operational data and leverage information only available at runtime
Initially, research on self-adaptive systems leveraged RL algorithms that represent learned knowledge as a value function
Tabular approaches for value functions exhibit key limitations
Deep RL addresses disadvantages by representing learned knowledge as a neural network
